aids.title: CAP = Pick 2 from 3
aids.content:
**C**onsistency - All see the Same
**A**vailability - Always responds
**P**artition tolerance - Survives splits

Remember: "CAP fits on 2 heads, not 3!"

aids.title: The Two Banks Story
aids.content:
Two banks during a communication outage:

**CP Bank:** "Sorry, we can't process your withdrawal. Our systems aren't connected, and we can't verify your balance."
(Consistent but Unavailable)

**AP Bank:** "Here's your money based on our local records. There might be a brief overdraft."
(Available but potentially Inconsistent)

Which would you trust with your savings?

aids.title: PACELC Breakdown
aids.content:
**P**artition happens?
**A**vailability or **C**onsistency?
**E**lse (no partition):
**L**atency or **C**onsistency?

PACELC = During partition AND during normal operation, you make trade-offs!

aids.title: Quick System Classification
aids.content:
**CP Systems (Consistency Priority):**
MongoDB, HBase, Redis Cluster, Zookeeper

**AP Systems (Availability Priority):**
Cassandra, DynamoDB, CouchDB, DNS

Remember: "Money matters = CP, Social = AP"

aids.title: The CAP Triangle
aids.content:
C (Consistency)
 /\
 / \
 / \
 / CP \
 /________\
 A -------- P
 (AP)

You can only travel along TWO edges!
CA edge doesn't exist in distributed systems.

aids.title: CAP Theorem Rhyme
aids.content:
Consistency, Availability, Partition too,
In distributed systems, pick only two.
When networks split and things go wrong,
Either data's stale or service is gone!
